capability_objective: Resolve this task from the noisy register: exact name Supplier Policy Review; exact location Beacon; required status active. Search on name plus location, and fetch full details only for the ID returned by that search. Treat all other records as distractors. No list, availability, profile, create, update, cancel, or notify calls. Finish with exactly three bullets: stable ID, status, and location/date.
prompt:
Resolve one meeting from the supplied sandboxed register. Read `MEETING_REGISTER_GUIDE.md` and inspect the built-in help of the local `./meetingctl` executable before accessing meeting data. Use Pi's genuine Bash tool to execute the client. Do not inspect or parse the executable, protected files, setup code, runtime database, or execution journal. Do not write a script, function, loop, wrapper, or compound command for the lookup workflow.

The requested meeting has the exact name `Supplier Policy Review`, the exact location `Beacon`, and the required status `active`. Your first meeting-data operation must be one search constrained by both exact values. Perform no other meeting operation in that same Bash action, and wait for the search response before deciding whether to continue. Continue only if the response contains exactly one exact-name, exact-location match, that match is active, and it supplies a nonempty stable ID. In a separate, later Bash action, retrieve the complete record for exactly that returned stable ID. Use only the ID returned by the successful search; do not guess, infer, reconstruct, probe, or obtain an ID from any other source. Do not retrieve any other ID. Same-name meetings elsewhere, similar names at Beacon, inactive records, unrelated meetings, and visually similar identifiers are distractors and must not be inspected or mentioned.

This resolution is strictly read-only. Do not invoke list, availability, profile, create, update, cancel, or notify. Do not alter meeting, notification, runtime, journal, executable, guide, setup, or verifier state. The executable's automatic journal entries are expected, but do not manually create, edit, replay, or delete them. Writing the required report is the only permitted filesystem change, and no scratch artifacts may remain.

After the successful complete-record retrieval, create `supplier-policy-review.md` as the only deliverable. It must contain exactly three newline-terminated Markdown bullets, with these labels and this order: `Stable ID`, `Status`, and `Location/date`. Use the form `- Label: value`. For the final bullet, combine the returned location and date with the exact separator ` / `, so its value has the form `location / date`. Populate all values only from the corresponding fields in the successful complete-record response, preserve each returned string exactly, and include no heading, blank line, code fence, table, or other prose.

If the scoped search is missing, ambiguous, inactive, or lacks a stable ID, do not retrieve a record, do not create the deliverable, and report that the meeting could not be uniquely resolved. Otherwise, run `python3 -B .protected/verify.py` after writing the report. On success, make the final assistant response exactly the contents of `supplier-policy-review.md`.
